channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump hits Colombian President Petro with sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration leveled sanctions directly on Colombian President Gustavo Petro and his family members, escalating his feud with the South American leader.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: A long-hidden Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar has sold for 32 million euros at an auction in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Oleg Cetinic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkish court dismisses case challenging main opposition party's congress
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A Turkish court on Friday dismissed a case challenging the legitimacy of the main opposition party’s 2023 internal election, ruling that there was no legal basis to remove the current leadership from office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuanian president says Russian military planes violated the Baltic country's airspace
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Russian military planes briefly violated Lithuania’s airspace Thursday evening, the Lithuanian president said, condemning what he called a blatant breach of the territorial integrity of his European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Lithuanian president says Russian military planes violated the Baltic country's airspace
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Russian military planes briefly violated Lithuania’s airspace Thursday evening, the Lithuanian president said, condemning what he called a blatant breach of the territorial integrity of his European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says declaration of war not needed for strikes on alleged cartel boats
item.description: President Trump on Thursday dismissed the need to ask Congress for a declaration of war as the U.S. military continuously launches strikes on boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean. “I don’t think we’re going to necessarily ask for a declaration of war,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III prays with pope in historic visit, a welcome respite from royal troubles at home
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — King Charles III and Queen Camilla prayed Thursday with Pope Leo XIV in an historic visit to the Vatican to forge closer relations between the Church of England and the Catholic Church, a welcome spiritual respite for the royals from the turmoil at home over sexual misconduct all
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China to focus on speeding up self-reliance in science and tech in new economic plan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s ruling Communist Party said Thursday it will focus on speeding up self-reliance in science and technology, a long-running push that has become more pronounced as the U.S. has imposed increasingly tight controls on access to semiconductors and other high-tech items.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, HUIZHONG WU and CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top UN court says Israel must allow UN relief agency to supply aid to Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Court of Justice said on Wednesday that Israel must allow the U.N. aid agency in Gaza, known as UNRWA, to provide humanitarian assistance to the Palestinian territory. The Hague-based court was asked late last year by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione brought up Brian Thompson's name while in Asia: Acquaintance
item.description: Christian Sacchini noted Luigi Mangione was shocked by how little an MRI scan cost in Thailand as opposed to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US carries out 8th strike on alleged drug vessel, this time in Eastern Pacific, Hegseth says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another strike on a suspected drug vessel, this time in the Eastern Pacific, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as Trump prepares for South Korea visit
item.description: SEOUL, Oct 22 (Reuters) - North Korea fired what appeared to be multiple short-range ballistic missiles on Wednesday, South Korea's military said, a week before a key Asia-Pacific leaders' meeting in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kim and Heejin Kim, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pick for religious freedom envoy visits Holy Land, cites strong US-Israel bond
item.description: Baptist pastor Mark Walker discusses U.S.-Israel spiritual bond and plans to combat global religious persecution after Trump nomination as international religious freedom ambassador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Louvre burglars obtained truck-mounted lift to make off with jewels worth more than $100M
item.description: Thieves used a stolen truck-mounted moving lift to scale the Louvre and steal royal jewels worth over $100 million in a lightning-fast Paris heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles days before Trump's visit to the peninsula
item.description: North Korea test fired ballistic missiles days before President Donald Trump's Asia trip to meet China's Xi Jinping and other leaders in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance meets with Netanyahu, urges patience as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: After meeting with Israel's prime minister, Vance said the United States' role was to support and safeguard the ceasefire, not to "monitor a toddler.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US military opens coordination center in Israel for Gaza stabilization and aid efforts amid ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. military created a Civil-Military Coordination Center near Gaza to oversee ceasefire implementation and facilitate humanitarian assistance from international partners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louvre director summoned by lawmakers after jewel theft
item.description: The Louvre is facing questions about lax security measures after thieves made off with priceless jewels.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kartalija, NewsNation

item.title: Experts urge Trump to ban terror-linked UN agency from his Gaza peace plan
item.description: Mideast experts urge excluding UNRWA from Gaza reconstruction under President Trump's peace plan, citing the agency's alleged Hamas connections and incompetence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former French President Sarkozy begins a 5-year prison sentence for campaign finance conspiracy
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy becomes the first modern French president imprisoned after conviction for criminal conspiracy involving Libya campaign financing in 2007 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says fragile Gaza ceasefire plan going better than expected
item.description: The U.S.-brokered deal has wavered amid renewed violence and uncertainty over next steps toward long-term peace.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine and EU leaders accuse Putin of stalling, reject land concessions for peace
item.description: They accused Russian President Vladimir Putin of stalling for time in diplomatic efforts to bring his invasion of Ukraine to an end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation
